subject: Re: Centring Tomograms in WARP Reconstruction
body:
Hi Katie,
Happy new year! 🥳 
Control over tomogram centering is only implicitly exposed via the ability to import tilt series alignments - we don’t expose it or provide any utilities for this.
Could you elaborate on why you would like this control? 
Cheers,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Jan 8, 2025, at 02:29, Katie Downes <katiegdownes@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Hello and Happy New Year Warpers!
How do you centre a tomogram in the reconstruction using warp? 
I have a batch of tomograms, the majority of which are centred, however one is too low and the other is too high. I'm not sure how to tinker with the reconstruction parameters in general given that the command doesn't have too many inputs. 
This is my command: WarpTools ts_reconstruct --settings warp_tiltseries.settings --angpix 10 --halfmap_frames --dont_invert
Any help or insight into this would be greatly appreciated! 
Many thanks, 
Dr Katie Downes 




--
